title: Listening - Part 3: Summer Courses
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a summer course they attended. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker gained from attending the course. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a summer course they attended. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker gained from attending the course. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about going on a summer course.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I attended a week-long course in engineering because I liked science subjects but had little idea what engineers actually do. We designed a small bridge, visited a company and heard people describe their jobs. [19]By the end, I understood the different kinds of engineering far better and had decided that environmental engineering was an area I seriously wanted to study at university.[/19] The course didn't make applications easier, of course, but now I have a direction rather than choosing a degree at random.||Speaker 2 Man: The drama course finished with a short performance for parents and local visitors. When I arrived on the first day, I avoided speaking loudly and hated the idea of strangers watching me. The teachers gradually gave us more challenging scenes. [20]After rehearsing all week, I walked onto the stage and delivered my lines clearly without the panic I normally feel when everybody is looking at me.[/20] I don't plan to become an actor, but presentations at school no longer seem quite so frightening.||Speaker 3 Woman: My parents signed me up for a basic cookery course because they said I couldn't live on toast when I moved away to study. At first I thought the week might be boring, but the tutor kept everything practical. [21]I learned to prepare several simple, affordable meals without following every instruction in a recipe, so I can now cook proper dinners for myself at home.[/21] I’ve even taken over one evening meal each week, which my parents seem extremely happy about.||Speaker 4 Man: I went on a Japanese language and culture course, mainly because I had enjoyed animated films and wanted to understand some words. Language classes were part of it, but there were also workshops on food, everyday manners and festivals. [22]The most important result was realising how much there was beyond the popular entertainment I already knew, and gaining a far deeper appreciation of how people live and communicate in Japan.[/22] I’ve continued studying the language, but my curiosity now goes much further than films.||Speaker 5 Woman: The outdoor adventure course included building shelters and completing navigation challenges. I expected to enjoy the climbing most, and I usually prefer doing tasks my own way. However, none of the activities could be completed alone. [23]I gradually learned to listen to other people’s suggestions, offer help instead of taking control and recognise that our group succeeded when everyone had a role.[/23] That was useful when I returned to school, because group projects no longer make me as impatient as they once did.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I attended a week-long course in engineering because I liked science subjects but had little idea what engineers actually do. We designed a small bridge, visited a company and heard people describe their jobs. By the end, I understood the different kinds of engineering far better and had decided that environmental engineering was an area I seriously wanted to study at university. The course didn't make applications easier, of course, but now I have a direction rather than choosing a degree at random.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: The drama course finished with a short performance for parents and local visitors. When I arrived on the first day, I avoided speaking loudly and hated the idea of strangers watching me. The teachers gradually gave us more challenging scenes. After rehearsing all week, I walked onto the stage and delivered my lines clearly without the panic I normally feel when everybody is looking at me. I don't plan to become an actor, but presentations at school no longer seem quite so frightening.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: My parents signed me up for a basic cookery course because they said I couldn't live on toast when I moved away to study. At first I thought the week might be boring, but the tutor kept everything practical. I learned to prepare several simple, affordable meals without following every instruction in a recipe, so I can now cook proper dinners for myself at home. I’ve even taken over one evening meal each week, which my parents seem extremely happy about.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I went on a Japanese language and culture course, mainly because I had enjoyed animated films and wanted to understand some words. Language classes were part of it, but there were also workshops on food, everyday manners and festivals. The most important result was realising how much there was beyond the popular entertainment I already knew, and gaining a far deeper appreciation of how people live and communicate in Japan. I’ve continued studying the language, but my curiosity now goes much further than films.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: The outdoor adventure course included building shelters and completing navigation challenges. I expected to enjoy the climbing most, and I usually prefer doing tasks my own way. However, none of the activities could be completed alone. I gradually learned to listen to other people’s suggestions, offer help instead of taking control and recognise that our group succeeded when everyone had a role. That was useful when I returned to school, because group projects no longer make me as impatient as they once did.

options.text: a clearer idea about a future career
a better understanding of another culture
a skill they could use at home
an opportunity to win an award
an improved ability to work in a group

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The course helped the speaker decide on a specific future study and career direction (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The drama course made the speaker more confident about appearing in front of an audience (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker developed cooking abilities that are useful in everyday home life (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The course expanded the speaker's understanding of life and customs in Japan (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The course taught the speaker how to cooperate more effectively in group tasks (evidence [23]).
